commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Things, as I have done respecting Cyrus? (St. Cyril) who was a figure of Christ, ver. 15.

commentaries.author: Jerome
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh All you, assemble yourselves, and hear; who among them has declared these things. The LORD has loved him: he will do his pleasure on Babylon, and his arm shall be on the Chaldeans. According to the Septuagint “to remove the seed of the Chaldeans” refers to the person of the Lord, who was indeed beloved of his Father and who did the will of his Father completely and who turned over all the seed of the Babylonians—which means the demons in Babylon, that is, those who lead the world into confusion.
